## reference · ux data tables scannable numbers
* Tables are suitable for large multivariate datasets and support four main user tasks: finding records, comparing data, viewing/editing a single row, and taking actions on records.
* To support finding records, use a human-readable record identifier in the first column, order columns by importance, and provide discoverable, quick, and powerful filters.
* For comparing data, ensure users can keep track of what they're looking at by freezing header rows and columns, and use visual design elements like borders, zebra striping, and hover-triggered highlighting.
* To facilitate comparison, allow hiding and reordering of columns and records, and make sorting easy to accomplish.
* When viewing, editing, or adding a single row, consider using edit-in-place, non-modal panels or separate windows, or accordions, each with tradeoffs to consider.
* For taking action on records, implement single-record actions and batch actions, and consider placing single-record actions inline or in a dropdown menu.

Sources: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/data-tables/
